prompt: TouchDesigner is RUNNING and the live tools are connected. I want visual proof that POP viewer capture works — POPs render through the op-viewer path and used to come back blank. Work entirely inside a fresh container you create at /eval_s16_scratch (touch nothing else in the project, and do NOT save the project at any point). Look up the Point Generator POP in the knowledge base first to get the real create token and parameter names. Then, inside the scratch container: create a Point Generator POP, give it a non-default point count so the capture is unambiguous, and capture its viewer with the universal operator-viewer capture tool. Tell me the point count and bounds that come back in the capture's sidecar — those numbers, not a guess from the image. Finally delete the whole /eval_s16_scratch container. Report: the captured point count, the bounds, and confirmation of cleanup.
notes: W-B regression scenario (PR #26): two-phase op-viewer capture for POPs (prime → one real UI frame → pull, op_viewer_warming handshake handled inside the tool) plus the POP numPoints/bounds sidecar. The tool_result_re binds to the sidecar caption ('POP points: N' / 'Points: N | Prims: ...') — a blank/partial capture has no sidecar numbers to report. The [^\n]{0,16} slack before the digit is deliberate: the 2026-07-14 live-verify filed BUG-B 'POP numPoints repr' (chip task_3afbf74d), so the count may render wrapped until that lands; the assertion still pins label + numeric content on one line. Born aspirational; td_live_running gated, auto-SKIPs elsewhere. v2 (2026-07-16): cleanup asserted as an OUTCOME via live.absent, replacing v1's `tool_called: delete_td_node` — same mechanism-over-outcome defect that failed s15 on its first live run (see s15 notes). s16 only ever passed because the agent happened to reach for delete_td_node; the .destroy() path would have failed it identically.
